items.title: No charges for Mark Bird, who Tasmanian coroner found faked wife Helen's suicide
items.summary: A man who a coroner found made his wife's 2010 death look like a suicide will not face prosecution after police said there was "insufficient evidence" to lay charges.

items.title: Influential Aboriginal leader Lowitja O'Donoghue immortalised in bronze statue
items.summary: A bronze statue of influential Aboriginal leader Lowitja O'Donoghue has been unveiled in Adelaide — the first of six monuments of prominent Indigenous South Australians that the state government has planned.

items.title: Plan to relocate Robertson's Big Potato rejected by Wingecarribee Council
items.summary: One of Australia's most infamous "big things" could be moved to make way for a new supermarket in the NSW Southern Highlands.

items.title: What the looming sale of Melbourne Renegades means for fans and the BBL
items.summary: If the Renegades are sold to a private buyer as expected, what could it mean for Melbourne fans if the BBL team no longer looks like itself?

items.title: Paedophile Peter Liddy's release from jail prompts vigilante warning from police
items.summary: South Australia's police chief says the force will respond accordingly to concerns about public safety once paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is released from prison, but added there would be no tolerance for "vigilante activity".

items.title: Duo arrested following alleged crime spree around Mildura and cross-border pursuit
items.summary: Victorian police say two people have been arrested over an alleged crime spree and cross-border pursuit in the Sunraysia region.

items.title: Labor senator Raff Ciccone swearing in Senate Estimates

items.title: Underworld figure Mick Gatto released without charge following arrest
items.summary: Mick Gatto has been released without charge after being arrested by Victorian police investigating "alleged financial offences" earlier today.

items.title: Pair found guilty of killing Greenbushes man Raymond Smith during home burglary
items.summary: Two men are convicted of killing 68-year-old Raymond Smith in his own home in Western Australia's South West during a meth-addled burglary for drugs.

items.title: SA 2026–27 state budget to include funding for treaty commission, cuts to public service
items.summary: Cuts to South Australia's public service and new funding for a treaty commission have been announced as the state Labor government gets set to reveal its budget on Thursday.

items.title: Reece Walsh opens up on Maroons omission for State of Origin I against Blues
items.summary: Reece Walsh says he understands why he was left out of Queensland's State of Origin squad for the series opener.

items.title: Authorities 'deeply sorry' for teenager let down by WA's mental health system
items.summary: The WA health minister and director of the state's country health service have unreservedly apologised to a 17-year-old left in an emergency room for 116 hours waiting for specialised mental health care.

items.title: Border Force busts multi-million-dollar illicit tobacco operation in the Northern Territory
items.summary: Australian Border Force officers have seized hundreds of thousands of illegal cigarettes and vapes worth millions of dollars from a Northern Territory storage facility.

items.title: Five ways to prevent mould in your home and the best ways to clean it
items.summary: Winter is here and with it comes the increased risk of mould in homes, particularly in colder parts of the country. But there are simple ways to keep to it at bay.

items.title: 1 million tonnes of fertiliser imported into Australia since Iran war began
items.summary: Streamlined biosecurity checks of fertiliser shipments have fast-tracked imports of urea for Australia's winter crop.

items.title: Fremantle Dockers AFL player Nathan O'Driscoll says mental health admission 'best thing' he did
items.summary: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll says he has received "nothing but love" after posting on social media about his struggles with anxiety and how he contemplated taking his own life.

items.title: Solomon Islands agrees to treaty negotiation with Australia
items.summary: The new Solomon Islands PM has made a clear pivot towards Australia, agreeing to kickstart negotiations on a comprehensive treaty and promising to "review" the Pacific nation's contentious security agreement with China.

items.title: Australia's economy growing at 2.5 per cent annually as slowdown begins
items.summary: Australia's economy grew at an annual rate of 2.5 per cent in the March quarter, the same as in the previous quarter.

items.title: Angry swarm of bees surrounds hive truck rollover on Warrego Highway west of Roma
items.summary: Angry bees are buzzing around a truck carrying hives after it crashed on the Warrego Highway this morning.

items.title: Defamation case involving former Carlton president Luke Sayers to be heard publicly after failed court bid
items.summary: A bid by Mr Sayers to have a defamation case brought by his wife over a nude photo scandal heard privately has been rejected by a judge.

items.title: Iranian missiles strike Kuwait International Airport as US hits Qeshm Island
items.summary: At least one person has been killed and more than 60 injured in an attack on Kuwait's international airport, as Iran and the US launch strikes against each other.

items.title: Sunshine Coast Council increases rates and cuts 130 jobs as costs spiral
items.summary: Residents are angry after copping another 10 per cent rate rise as the council grapples with how to provide services for the fast-growing region.

items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: New political candidates to benefit under changes to Victoria's electoral act
items.summary: Six months out from the state election, Victoria had no rules governing donations due to the High Court finding in April that the laws were unconstitutional.

items.title: Sheepskin market dramatically rebounds amid tighter supply and Chinese demand
items.summary: Just 12 months ago, Australian sheepskins were worthless and being dumped in landfill. They are now in hot demand amid a surging merino wool industry.

items.title: Barnaby Joyce speaks at Sydney rally ahead of bill to outlaw sex-selective abortions
items.summary: Nine months out from the NSW election campaign, One Nation recruit Barnaby Joyce has appeared in Sydney, declaring politicians are fearful of losing their jobs.

items.title: Queensland crackdown cuts illicit tobacco and vape shops, but black market adapts
items.summary: Queensland's crackdown has reduced visible illicit tobacco sales, according to a public health researcher, but the black market has adapted, prompting calls for the tobacco excise to be cut.

items.title: Protesters clash with police in Southampton over murder of Henry Nowak
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Medicare bulk-billing model forces Kalbarri Doctors Surgery closure, director says
items.summary: Kalbarri residents will be forced to travel more than 100 kilometres to visit a doctor when the regional WA town's only GP clinic closes due to financial pressure.

items.title: Former Canberra Liberals leader Leanne Castley leaves party, citing 'toxic culture, threat of physical assault'
items.summary: Former Canberra Liberals leader Leanne Castley says she has experienced "bullying, intimidation, lies and a threat of physical assault" in the past 18 months.

items.title: Young cancer patient's stem cell transplant struggle highlights shortage of local donors
items.summary: Sarah Taylor is one of approximately 1,200 Australians urgently waiting for a life-saving stem cell transplant. Advocates say Australia's registry is "ridiculously small" compared to other nations, leading to potentially fatal consequences.

items.title: Australia levels ODI series in Pakistan as Nathan Ellis stars with the ball
items.summary: Nathan Ellis shines with the ball, while Cameron Green tops the run-scoring for Australia in a 41-run ODI victory over Pakistan in Lahore.

items.title: Red skies, rainbows and a ravaging storm — see WA's best weather photos for May
items.summary: May delivered Western Australia bursts of sunshine, brief spells of rain and long stretches of dry skies that left many wondering when winter would finally arrive — until it did with a bang.

items.title: Starmer condemns Henry Nowak's death as police response sparks Southampton race protests
items.summary: The British prime minister said he "felt sick" while watching video released by police of 18-year-old Henry Nowak, who was handcuffed while he lay dying from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack.

items.title: Bodycam vision shows UK police handcuffing stabbed man
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Australian Opal Centre receives $1 million government lifeline as it scales back to survive
items.summary: Construction of the museum boasting the world's greatest public collection of opalised dinosaur fossils has been on hold since the project ran out of money last year.

items.title: PM labels Taylor 'Temu Abbott' as Coalition turns up heat on economy — as it happened
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese faces a grilling from the opposition over Australia's economy after new figures show a slowdown in growth.

items.title: Marta Kostyuk praises Daria Kasatkina ahead of French Open semifinal against Russia's Mirra Andreeva
items.summary: Ukrainian star Marta Kostyuk says it is unacceptable for Russians to stay silent on the war, especially after Daria Kasatkina's defection to Australia.

items.title: Reports of wildlife displaced at Fred Caterson Reserve as controversial sports project begins
items.summary: A sports development in Sydney's north-west has been accused by residents of killing and displacing wildlife.

items.title: Mango twig tip dieback spreading in the NT as more trees get bulldozed
items.summary: At a field day in the Northern Territory, mango growers were desperate to learn what could be done to stop the spread of mango twig tip dieback.

items.title: SA Voice damaged by advertising failures and voter confusion, FOI documents show
items.summary: New documents suggest South Australia's inaugural Voice to Parliament election was hamstrung by advertising failures that left First Nations voters confused or unaware an election was taking place.

items.title: Quilpie builders experiment with 'flat-pack' housing to improve supply in the outback
items.summary: A Queensland outback town pins its hopes of solving a local housing crisis on "flat-pack" homes as the high cost of building risks paralysing the community.

items.title: Tasmanians letting fitness regimes slip as cost-of-living pressures rise
items.summary: As household budgets tighten, keeping up a health and fitness regime often slips, but experts say giving people cheap or free ways to stay fit is critical to longer-term wellbeing.

items.title: Billions over budget and years late: How Snowy 2.0 got here
items.summary: Australia's most ambitious renewable energy project is slowly nearing completion, but experts are divided on whether Snowy 2.0 will ever justify its snowballing price tag.

items.title: Tasmanian premier rules out investigation into leaked personal email request
items.summary: Jeremy Rockliff won't investigate how an email containing the health-related information of a politician was leaked to the media and suggests she could take up the matter with a complaints body that doesn't exist yet.

items.title: We asked Google AI about big tobacco. Its answer was missing some controversial history
items.summary: We searched Google for information on some of the world's biggest tobacco companies. The results raised questions about how much of the AI overview was coming from the brands themselves.

items.title: Australians have become housing hostages. Will the budget set them free?
items.summary: Before the federal budget, the housing market was already starting to choke on its own excesses. Now some economists are tipping an extended period where home prices grow no faster than general inflation.

items.title: Three Australian men found guilty after taking 'black flight' into Indonesia's South Papua province
items.summary: Three Australian men have been found guilty by a District Court in Merauke after taking a "black flight" into Indonesia's South Papua province from Far North Queensland without visas.

items.title: Australian-made rocket motor designed for missiles test-fired for first time
items.summary: In what is being described as a significant leap forward in Australia's push to develop and manufacture its own missiles, newly released footage shows the first test-firing of a locally made solid rocket motor.

items.title: Almost eight months on from the ceasefire, Gaza's residents are still struggling to rebuild a city in ruins
items.summary: It's been seven months since the ceasefire in Gaza, but reconstruction has barely begun, water remains contaminated and minimal aid is getting into the strip. This is what life is like for its residents.

items.title: Ralph Jackman's memoir about his time as a teacher in a youth detention centre reveals a broken system
items.summary: After Ralph Jackman retrained as a teacher, his first job was in a youth detention centre. His new memoir reveals how a broken system is failing vulnerable kids.

items.title: Travel quiz, June 3: Hold on to your hat, we're off on a tour of the globe
items.summary: The famous US road trip along Route 66 links which two cities? And which country eats the most garlic per person?

items.title: Gender euphoria behind the mic: Kae Tempest refuses to let transphobia slow him down
items.summary: Poet, rapper, musician, playwright and novelist Kae Tempest is a prodigious polymath. It wasn't until he affirmed his gender that he connected with his true self.

items.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon amid ceasefire with Hezbollah
items.summary: Lebanese state media has reported Israeli strikes, some deadly, at about 30 locations in the country's south despite a partial ceasefire.

items.title: UN warns countries to prepare for moderate to strong El Niño amid deadly heatwave in India
items.summary: India's deadly heatwave comes as the UN warns the world to prepare for a moderate to strong El Niño this year.

items.title: Melbourne Renegades BBL licence to be sold as Cricket Victoria establishes new team
items.summary: The Melbourne Renegades BBL licence is set to be sold to a private investor, with Cricket Victoria to establish a new team, marking the end of the Stars as well.

items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Coroner questions parents on use of mobile phone, early morning laundry during infant death inquest
items.summary: A coronial inquest is being held into the death of 18-week-old, premature-born twins, who cannot be identified for legal reasons, who died in their Far North Queensland home in late 2021.

items.title: One Nation SA says it will not support Labor's bills as it seeks more parliamentary resources
items.summary: South Australia's One Nation has vowed to block every Labor government bill following a dispute over staffing allocations.

items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Katter attempts to force discussion on abortion access in Queensland parliament despite ban on debate
items.summary: The subject became an issue ahead of the last Queensland election in October 2024, with Premier David Crisafulli implementing a ban on abortion debate after winning government.
